body.question_1: Reflecting on the full academic year, how would you rate your overall educational experience at this university?
body.answer_1: Reflecting on the full academic year in the Data Science program at the School of Information Technology, I can say with confidence that this has been an remarkable and transformative educational experience that has exceeded my expectations in most meaningful ways. The combination of rigorous coursework, dedicated faculty, strong peer community, and access to industry connections has equipped me with both the technical competencies and professional mindset needed to succeed in my chosen field. I have grown significantly as a learner and feel genuinely prepared for the next stage of my academic or professional journey. I recognize the institution for maintaining high standards throughout and look forward to continuing my studies here with great enthusiasm.

body.question_2: How effectively do you believe this year's coursework has prepared you for your future career or further academic studies?
body.answer_2: Career preparation services at the School of Information Technology, offered through the Career Center, are acceptable and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_3: How would you assess the progression of your academic skills and knowledge throughout this academic year?
body.answer_3: The Data Science curriculum at the School of Information Technology appears to lag behind current industry standards in several significant areas that I believe require urgent review and revision. Many courses continue to teach outdated tools and methodologies that are rarely used in professional settings today, leaving students underprepared for actual workplace demands. The curriculum also lacks sufficient elective depth and specialization pathways, limiting students' ability to develop expertise in niche areas of growing importance. I flag the Academic Affairs Office and school leadership to conduct a thorough curriculum audit in close consultation with industry professionals and alumni to ensure course content remains relevant, rigorous, and career-oriented.

body.question_4: How satisfied are you with the extracurricular activities, student clubs, and personal development opportunities available?
body.answer_4: Extracurricular activities at the School of Information Technology offer a acceptable range of options that serve the interests of a portion of the student body but do not yet reflect the full diversity of student interests and backgrounds. Core clubs and major university events are reasonably well-organized and attended, but support for emerging student initiatives is limited and the process for establishing new clubs is unnecessarily bureaucratic. Increasing the budget allocated to student activities, streamlining club registration procedures, and investing in purpose-built student activity spaces would encourage broader participation and strengthen the campus culture for the benefit of all enrolled students.

body.question_5: How well has the university's Career Center prepared you for internship opportunities and graduate employment?
body.answer_5: Career preparation services at the School of Information Technology, offered through the Career Center, are decent and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_6: How would you rate the university's financial services, including tuition fee management and scholarship support?
body.answer_6: The financial services provided by the Finance Department at the School of Information Technology have been outstanding this semester, and I genuinely praise the clarity, transparency, and efficiency with which they are managed. Fee statements are issued promptly and accurately, with clear breakdowns of tuition, facility charges, and any applicable scholarship deductions that make it easy to verify and plan payments. Payment options are flexible and well-communicated, including installment plans that meaningfully reduce financial pressure on students and families. Scholarship disbursements are processed on time and confirmed through official notifications, removing uncertainty around critical financial support. The finance office staff are professional, patient, and genuinely helpful when navigating complex financial queries.

body.question_7: How would you evaluate the IT infrastructure and digital services provided by the university over the course of the year?
body.answer_7: The IT infrastructure at the School of Information Technology, maintained by the IT Support Department, performs at a acceptable level that meets basic student needs but falls short of what is required to fully support a modern, technology-dependent academic environment. Wi-Fi connectivity is generally stable in primary teaching spaces but becomes inconsistent during peak usage hours and in peripheral campus locations. Software availability in computer labs is adequate for core coursework but limited for students requiring specialized applications for advanced projects. Expanding bandwidth capacity, updating hardware on a regular replacement cycle, and improving helpdesk responsiveness would meaningfully elevate the digital learning experience for the entire student community.

body.question_8: How satisfied are you with the library resources — both physical and digital — that were available throughout the academic year?
body.answer_8: The learning materials provided for several of my Data Science courses at the School of Information Technology are problematic and fall short of what students need to succeed academically. Required textbooks are outdated and frequently out of stock in both the campus bookstore and the library, forcing students to source materials independently at considerable expense and inconvenience. The Learning Management System is inconsistently used by lecturers — some post materials days after class, and some modules have no digital resources whatsoever. I strongly urge the Library Services and IT Support Department to collaborate on ensuring all course resources are accessible digitally, current, and available before the semester commences.

body.question_9: How effectively did the university communicate important information, deadlines, and updates throughout the academic year?
body.answer_9: The academic administrative processes managed by the Academic Affairs Office at the School of Information Technology have been excellent this semester, and I value the efficiency and transparency with which they are handled. Course registration was smooth and intuitive via the student portal, with clear instructions provided well in advance of enrollment deadlines. Grade appeals and transcript requests are processed promptly and communicated through official channels with regular status updates that reduce student anxiety. Administrative staff are approachable, knowledgeable, and genuinely helpful when students encounter difficulties navigating university policies or procedures. The overall administrative experience reflects a student-centered approach that I believe enhances the broader university experience significantly.

body.question_10: What are your key recommendations for improving the overall student experience in the coming academic year?
body.answer_10: My main recommendations for improving the Data Science student experience at the School of Information Technology center on consistency, communication, and investment in areas that currently deliver only reasonable outcomes. First, standardizing teaching quality, assessment practices, and resource provision across all modules would significantly reduce the frustration caused by the wide variability students currently experience between courses. Second, improving the speed and transparency of administrative and financial communications would reduce unnecessary anxiety and wasted time for students. Finally, expanding industry engagement through internship pipelines, career workshops, and alumni networking events would better equip graduates for the increasingly competitive job market they will enter upon graduation.
